pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT APPLICATION FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word or ODS. The more information
you provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have any
questions with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me.
You can use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you
prefer to fill in this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb,
inch).

General
 Full legal name: D
 Age: 31
 Height: 5' 10"
 Weight: 215.3 lbs
 Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 9.9%
(measured via BodPod on 10/9/15)
 Years of training experience: Intermediate (lifting for ~17 years
total although with many breaks and poor coaching through much of
that time)

Strength
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in
weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press: 335 lbs x 3
 Squat (excluding body weight): 405 lbs x 3
 Chin-up (excluding body weight): (BW + 90 lbs) x 4

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 Overhead press: 225 lbs x 2

What are your fitness goals?
1. Bodybuilding stage ready ASAP. I just recently got down to 10% and
need help getting the rest of the way down to 4-6% to be stage-ready.
2. Then I would like to maintain at no more than 4 weeks out from stage-
ready indefinitely (assuming this is possible).
3. Then bring up lagging areas to ideal proportions (e.g. upper arm
smaller than calves right now).
4. Finally I have a few strength goals that I'd like to hit at some point
(i.e. bench 400 lbs, squat 500 lbs, deadlift 600 lbs, chins 20 reps).

How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was
the optimal training frequency?
To get down to 10% I was lifting 5 days per week for 1.5 hours. And I was
doing MISS cardio @ ~135 bpm for 1 hour fasted in the morning and
another 1 hour after lifting or before bed 7 days per week. This was under
my old bodybuilding coach. This was torture. I would prefer to train less
but I am prepared to train as much if needed. So ~3 hours per day max.

What is your occupation?
You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
I am a technology planner for Apple (e.g. what is the screen in the next
iPhone going to be like?). My natural circadian rhythm would be sleeping
2am to 10am but generally end up doing midnight to 6am.
headings: How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
Diagnosed with low testosterone a few years ago (i.e. down in the low
200s). I had never Also hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue. Have since been
on testosterone injections, exemestane, and some version of thyroid med
(Armour, T3, or T3/T4 compounded). These have brought my levels into
"normal-high" range (e.g. test is now ~900). Before this I had never
taken any PEDs so I have come to believe all of my levels were destroyed
through stress and abuse of thermogenic when I was bulking/cutting in
high school and college for sports.

Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:
Other than those above I had been recommended to take ECA, yohimbine,
and HGH-Fragment by my old coach. I suppose I don't really consider
those PEDs although some countries believe otherwise. I am not
particularly for or against any such additional things but generally prefer
to be as natural as possible for fear of screwing up the tenuous balance I
have now.
headings: Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
(This includes when you're at work!) If so, please list them:
My work schedule is somewhat flexible so I can train almost anytime.
Ideal to avoid 9-5pm if possible due to normal work hours.

Activity level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Sedentary (e.g. office job)

Stress level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Average stress (e.g. full-time work with deadlines and commuting)

At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price
quote before filling in the rest. Please attach at least two full-body pictures
of yourself in clear lighting, preferably including a back double biceps and front
relaxed pose.

Legal disclaimer: By returning this intake form, you declare that while Menno
Henselmans is your personal trainer, all the substances you use are obtained
and used in accordance with their governing laws and you forego all rights to
hold Menno Henselmans liable for any damages caused in relation to following
his advice as your trainer.

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
Rhythm is somewhat off but generally midnight to 6am works. Longer on
the weekends. Quality is poor as I routinely wake up 3 times to urinate at
night due to high fluid intake around lifting at night (pre-workout (Evogen
EVP) and post-workout (Evogen Cell KEM and whey) shakes).

How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a
typical work day?
A lot. Caffeine pills before fasted cardio (400mg). ECA pill at lunch. Large
coffee in the morning. Sometimes a tea or more coffee before the gym at
night.

Genetics
 Wrist circumference: 18.4 cm (left) and 18.3 cm (right)
 Ankle circumference: 25.4 cm (left) and 25.3 cm (right)
 How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was
your height at that point? ~200 lbs at 12 years old before starting
wrestling and began some lifting. Weight came down to 170 lbs and
then I have been between 170 and 230 lbs ever since either from
lifting or cutting. Set point seems to really like 210 - 220 lbs.
 Were you heavy as a baby? Yes, always the fat kid :/
 Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right
hand is clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat
surface with your fingers touching each other and then take a
picture of it.
headings: How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a

2.5 lbs

to purchase
 powerlifting chains? No

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 a seated calf raise and if so, is it closed chain where you push your
body up (see example image below) or different? Yes, but different
where I am stationary and only the weight move up and down

Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?
Office gym so really nothing too specially good or bad about it. Extremely
busy so it can be more difficult to keep ideal pace or perform super sets

Supplements
Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
Supplements
•
Pre-Cardio (morning):

◦
2x Evogen Cell KEM

◦
1x Warrior Greens

◦
1x Evogen Carnigen

◦
2x Evogen Lipocide / 8x Primaforce Yohimbine HCL + 6x L-
Tyrosine
•
Mid-day = 1x ECA + 325mg Aspirin
•
Pre-workout = 2x EVP
•
Post-workout = 2x Cell KEM + 2x Hydrowhey

ECA, yohimbine, and HGH-Fragment

Current Program
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Most recent plan I used to go from 12% to 10% over 6 weeks:
Weekly Diet Schedule
•
2x protein / veggie days
•
1x ketogenic days
•
2x protein / veggie days
•
1x ketogenic days
•
1x high anabolic day

Protein / Veggie Day
•
1x egg + 2x cups egg whites
•
5x 9oz lean protein

Ketogenic Day
•
3x egg + 1x cup egg whites
•
2x 7oz 93% ground beef + 1 tsp MCT oil
•
2x 7oz chicken + 1/2 avo
•
1x 7oz fatty protein

High Anabolic Day
•
2x egg + 1.5x cups egg whites + Salsa + 2x servings oatmeal
•
1x 8oz fatty protein + 6oz Sweet Potato
•
3x 8oz lean protein + 6oz Sweet Potato
•
1x 8oz fatty protein + 6oz Sweet Potato
•
2x Evogen Glycoject (added to post-workout)

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
Day 1 - 7: Cardio
•
1 hour fasted cardio every morning @ ~135 bpm
•
1 hour cardio immediately after lifting (or before bed on non-lifting
days) @ ~135 bpm

Day 1: Chest / Triceps + Abs
•
60 seconds rest between sets
•
45% Incline Barbell bench press: 4 sets @ 12 / 10 / 8 / 6 reps
•
Superset:

◦
15% Incline Dumbell bench press: 3 sets @ 10 / 8 / 6 reps

◦
Incline cable fly: 3 sets @ 20 reps
•
Superset:

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

◦
Weighted dips: 3 sets @ 12 / 10 / 8 reps - all to failure

◦
Decline cable fly: 3 sets @ 20 reps
•
Close grip bench: 3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps
•
V handle triceps press down:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
elbows in
•
Overhead cable tri extension:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 10 / 8 reps

◦
elbows out, rope over shoulder, alternate each set

Day 2: Back / Biceps
•
60 seconds rest between sets
•
Back extension: warm-up lower back
•
Barbell row: 3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps
•
1-arm dumbbell row (or Unilateral Hammer Strength row):

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
bring weight down and back to hip flexing lower lat
•
Seated cable low row:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
use rope, split across abs, elbows down and low
•
Wide grip lat pull down:

◦
4 sets @ 12 / 10 / 8 / 6 reps

◦
chest up, shoulders back, from extended arms down to -
halfway down behind head
•
Close grip lat pull down:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
use V grip bar, chest up, shoulders back, from extended arms
down to touching chest
•
Straight arm pull over: 3 sets @ 12 / 11 / 10 reps
•
Barbell biceps curl:

◦
3 supersets @ 3 dropsets @ 10 reps

▪
e.g. 100 lbs failure @ 10 reps > 70 lbs fail @ 10 reps >
50 lbs fail @ 10 reps, then 8>8>8, then 6>6>6

◦
from almost extended up to highest point before flex
disengages
•
Unilateral dumbbell curl on incline bench - facing up:

◦
5 sets @ 5 reps (each arm on its own)

◦
e.g. 5 sets of 35 lbs @ 5 reps right arm then 5 reps left arm
•
Hammer curl - standing:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
bring weight across body; maintain hammer grip throughout

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Day 3: Legs
•
90 - 180 seconds rest between sets
•
Leg Extensions:

◦
3 sets @ 20 reps

◦
only warming up
•
Leg Press:

◦
3 sets @ 20 reps

◦
feet & knees straight, knees rotated into focus on outer
quads; set back on most reclined option to reduce hip rotation
•
Prone Leg Curls:

◦
3 sets @ 15 - 12 reps

◦
constant tension on hamstrings
•
Abductor Machine: 3 sets @ 15 - 12 reps
•
Adductor Machine: 3 sets @ 15 - 12 reps
•
Squat:

◦
4 sets @ 15 / 14 / 13 / 12 reps

◦
focus on pump in glutes not joint pain, take wider stance than
normal, stop short of bottom, don't bounce

◦
moved to end of workout to reduce back strain
•
Seated Calf Raises:

◦
3 sets @ 30 reps

◦
change to "dirty 30": 30 reps with necessary pauses, focus on
pinky toe to develop outside of calves
•
Standing Calf Raises: 3 sets @ 30 reps (dirty 30)

Day 4: Rest

Day 5: Shoulders
•
60 seconds rest between sets
•
Dumbbell shoulder press:

◦
4 sets @ 12 / 10 / 8 / 6 reps

◦
straight out, elbows to 90 degrees

◦
can move reps up to reduce weights -> 4 sets @ 16 / 14 / 12
/ 10
•
Close grip front shoulder press:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
perform as Arnolds, focus on front of anterior delt
•
Dumbbell side laterals:

◦
3 supersets @ 4 sets @ 10 reps

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

▪
e.g. 50 lbs failure @ 10 reps > 35 @ 10 > 25 @ 10 > 15
@ 10

◦
elbows up & bent, wrists slightly down & rotated
•
Dumbbell front raises:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
arm straight, weight brought up in front of face slightly across
body
•
Upright rows wide grip:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
bring to pec line, wide enough grip to hit 90 degrees with
elbows at top of rep, isolate traps out
•
Dumbbell shrugs:

◦
3 sets @ 12 / 9 / 6 reps

◦
on incline bench, head forward, flex shoulders up and into
traps not into neck

Day 6: Arms
•
60 seconds rest between sets
•
Standing Bicep Curl (Bent-Bar / Close-Grip):

◦
3 sets @ 21's (7 bottom, 7 top, 7 full ROM)

◦
e.g. 65 lbs @ 3 sets @ 21's
•
Preacher Bicep Curl (Bent-Bar / Close-Grip):

◦
3 sets @ 10 reps @ double pumped

◦
flex to top, drop 1-inch, flex again to top

◦
weight ~= 10% less than normal preacher curls
•
Standing Bicep Curl (Bent-Bar / Wide-Grip):

◦
Century set w/ 1st failure @ 50 reps

◦
afterwards rest-pause to 100 reps

◦
e.g. 50 lbs
•
Tricep Superset:

◦
Skull Crusher: 3 sets @ 10 reps

◦
Close-Grip Bench Press: 3 sets @ failure following Skull
Crushers
•
Behind-Head Rope Extensions:

◦
4 sets @ 10 reps - blood-starving (don't drop arms until the
end)

◦
place pin behind middle of back

◦
press overhead and separate arms
•
Tricep Dips:

◦
3 sets @ 10 reps @ double pumped

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Day 7: Rest

Abs:
•
no rotation - need to minimize obliques
•
Rope crunches

◦
30 reps

◦
setup like rope triceps, bring elbows to mid-quads
•
Superset decline bench crunches: 30 reps
•
Superset hanging leg raises: to failure
•
Complete the above tri-set for three rounds, every other workout -
for a total of 3x per week

So we've got an advanced bodybuilder that's strength training 7.5 hours a week and doing 2
hours of cardio a day. His hormone levels are normal, albeit via hormone replacement
therapy and he's on a crapload of stimulants in addition to growth hormone boosters. Yet
his fat loss plateaued at ~10% body fat (which was probably in retrospect in fact more like
15%). What gives?

Genetics are clearly not the cause, since his muscular potential is excellent and he doesn't
appear to even have maxed it out yet (and indeed he didn't, I can say in retrospect).

Here's what we did.
 We dropped all cardio.
 We ditched all the stimulants and growth hormone boosters. We reintroduced
caffeine, yohimbine and ephedrine at far lower dosages gradually after 9 days. (See
supplements topic.)
Please return the filled in form to
MennoHenselmans@BayesianBodybuilding.com and attach at least two
full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without excessive
shadows; not your flattering Facebook selfies), preferably including a
back double biceps and front relaxed pose. If you recently had blood
work done, please attach its results as well.

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 We increased caloric intake to 2799 a day: still 'low' carb but no longer ketogenic.
 We implemented high frequency training amounting to a training time of approx. one
hour a day.
 We focused greatly on stress management, improving his traveling habits (he traveled
a ton) and improving his sleep quality and time. Decreasing his meal frequency to just
3 meals a day and implementing intermittent morning fasting made his meal prep a lot
easier.

The result: He actually started recomping throughout the first month, which was beyond my
expectation and indicative of greatly above average muscular potential. His strength levels
increased by ~10% in this period.

Take-home message: Never underestimate the importance of stress and sleep.

Just so you don't get the wrong idea, after this period the recomping stopped and we still
had to decrease caloric intake and introduce cardio, as expected during contest prep.

--- Page 1 ---
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT APPLICATION FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word or ODS. The more information
you provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have any
questions with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me.
You can use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you
prefer to fill in this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb,
inch).

General
 Full legal name: D
 Age: 31
 Height: 5' 10"
 Weight: 215.3 lbs
 Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): 9.9%
(measured via BodPod on 10/9/15)
 Years of training experience: Intermediate (lifting for ~17 years
total although with many breaks and poor coaching through much of
that time)

Strength
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in
weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press: 335 lbs x 3
 Squat (excluding body weight): 405 lbs x 3
 Chin-up (excluding body weight): (BW + 90 lbs) x 4


--- Page 2 ---
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 Overhead press: 225 lbs x 2

What are your fitness goals?
1. Bodybuilding stage ready ASAP. I just recently got down to 10% and
need help getting the rest of the way down to 4-6% to be stage-ready.
2. Then I would like to maintain at no more than 4 weeks out from stage-
ready indefinitely (assuming this is possible).
3. Then bring up lagging areas to ideal proportions (e.g. upper arm
smaller than calves right now).
4. Finally I have a few strength goals that I'd like to hit at some point
(i.e. bench 400 lbs, squat 500 lbs, deadlift 600 lbs, chins 20 reps).

How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was
the optimal training frequency?
To get down to 10% I was lifting 5 days per week for 1.5 hours. And I was
doing MISS cardio @ ~135 bpm for 1 hour fasted in the morning and
another 1 hour after lifting or before bed 7 days per week. This was under
my old bodybuilding coach. This was torture. I would prefer to train less
but I am prepared to train as much if needed. So ~3 hours per day max.

What is your occupation?
You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
I am a technology planner for Apple (e.g. what is the screen in the next
iPhone going to be like?). My natural circadian rhythm would be sleeping
2am to 10am but generally end up doing midnight to 6am.


--- Page 3 ---
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
Diagnosed with low testosterone a few years ago (i.e. down in the low
200s). I had never Also hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue. Have since been
on testosterone injections, exemestane, and some version of thyroid med
(Armour, T3, or T3/T4 compounded). These have brought my levels into
"normal-high" range (e.g. test is now ~900). Before this I had never
taken any PEDs so I have come to believe all of my levels were destroyed
through stress and abuse of thermogenic when I was bulking/cutting in
high school and college for sports.

Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:
Other than those above I had been recommended to take ECA, yohimbine,
and HGH-Fragment by my old coach. I suppose I don't really consider
those PEDs although some countries believe otherwise. I am not
particularly for or against any such additional things but generally prefer
to be as natural as possible for fear of screwing up the tenuous balance I
have now.


--- Page 4 ---
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
(This includes when you're at work!) If so, please list them:
My work schedule is somewhat flexible so I can train almost anytime.

Rhythm is somewhat off but generally midnight to 6am works. Longer on

A lot. Caffeine pills before fasted cardio (400mg). ECA pill at lunch. Large

So we've got an advanced bodybuilder that's strength training 7.5 hours a week and doing 2
hours of cardio a day. His hormone levels are normal, albeit via hormone replacement
therapy and he's on a crapload of stimulants in addition to growth hormone boosters. Yet
his fat loss plateaued at ~10% body fat (which was probably in retrospect in fact more like
15%). What gives?

Genetics are clearly not the cause, since his muscular potential is excellent and he doesn't
appear to even have maxed it out yet (and indeed he didn't, I can say in retrospect).

Here's what we did.
 We dropped all cardio.
 We ditched all the stimulants and growth hormone boosters. We reintroduced
caffeine, yohimbine and ephedrine at far lower dosages gradually after 9 days. (See
supplements topic.)
Please return the filled in form to
MennoHenselmans@BayesianBodybuilding.com and attach at least two
full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without excessive
shadows; not your flattering Facebook selfies), preferably including a
back double biceps and front relaxed pose. If you recently had blood
work done, please attach its results as well.


--- Page 14 ---
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2015

 We increased caloric intake to 2799 a day: still 'low' carb but no longer ketogenic.
 We implemented high frequency training amounting to a training time of approx. one
hour a day.
 We focused greatly on stress management, improving his traveling habits (he traveled
a ton) and improving his sleep quality and time. Decreasing his meal frequency to just
3 meals a day and implementing intermittent morning fasting made his meal prep a lot
easier.

The result: He actually started recomping throughout the first month, which was beyond my
expectation and indicative of greatly above average muscular potential. His strength levels
increased by ~10% in this period.

Take-home message: Never underestimate the importance of stress and sleep.

Just so you don't get the wrong idea, after this period the recomping stopped and we still
had to decrease caloric intake and introduce cardio, as expected during contest prep.
